title: Demographic Transition Model
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive p5.js MicroSim showing how birth rates, death rates, and total population change through the four stages of demographic transition with real country examples.

educational.topic: Demographic Transition and Human Population Dynamics
learningObjectives: Explain how birth rates, death rates, and total population change through the four stages of demographic transition
Identify which stage real countries currently occupy and explain determining factors
Predict future population trends for countries at different stages
Analyze the relationship between economic development and population growth
prerequisites: Basic understanding of birth rate and death rate

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Animated timeline with real country examples
keyQuestions: In which stage does the most rapid population growth occur and why?
Why do death rates typically decline before birth rates?
What factors drive a country from one stage to the next?
What challenges do Stage 4 countries face with declining populations?
How does the demographic transition model help predict future resource needs?
commonMisconceptions: Population growth is the same in all countries
High population growth is caused by rising birth rates rather than falling death rates
All countries will eventually reach Stage 4 at the same pace
assessmentOpportunities: Stage identification exercise with real country data
Prediction writing for countries in transition
Comparison analysis of two countries at different stages
